questions.question: Which of the following provisions of the Indian Constitution deals with the distribution of powers between the Centre and the States?
questions.explanation: Article 246 outlines the distribution of legislative powers.

questions.question: The provision that prohibits a person from holding offices of profit under the State (other than an office of honour) is contained in?
questions.explanation: Article 191 deals with disqualifications for membership of the State Legislature.

questions.question: The West Bengal Panchayat and Municipalities Elections Laws (Amendment) Act, 2021 aimed at implementing the?
questions.explanation: The 105th Constitutional Amendment aimed to extend reservation for SC/STs to the panchayats and municipalities in West Bengal.

questions.question: According to the Panchayats Extension to the Scheduled Areas (PESA) Act, 1996, which of the following councils has to be constituted in the Scheduled Areas?
questions.explanation: A Gram Sabha is to be constituted in the Scheduled Areas under PESA.

questions.question: The concept of 'Nagarpalika' was first introduced by the?
questions.explanation: The 91st Constitutional Amendment Act, 2003 made provisions for the 'Nagarpalika' in India.

questions.question: Who led the Indian National Congress in Bengal during the Non-Cooperation Movement (1920-1922)?
questions.explanation: C.R. Das was a prominent leader of the Indian National Congress in Bengal.

questions.question: Which event took place at the Alfred Park in Kolkata on 21st August 1942?
questions.explanation: The hartal in Bengal was called on 21st August 1942.

questions.question: Which of the following was the main objective of the Swadeshi movement in Bengal during the early 20th century?
options: Encouragement of foreign investment in India
Support to the British colonial rule
questions.explanation: The Swadeshi movement aimed to boycott foreign goods.

questions.question: The partition of which of the following provinces in 1905 led to widespread protests in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The partition of Bengal in 1905 led to widespread protests.

questions.question: Who was the famous Bengali poet and writer who played an important role in the Indian freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay was a famous Bengali poet and writer.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers originates from the Himalayas and flows through the Darjeeling district in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Tista River originates from the Himalayas.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest island in the Sundarbans, West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Hollong Island is the largest island in the Sundarbans.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain passes is situated between Nepal and Darjeeling, West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Jelep La Pass is situated between Nepal and Darjeeling.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is the longest in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Ganges River is the longest in West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following deltas is located in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Ganges-Brahmaputra Delta is located in West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following is a flagship program under the Mamata Banerjee government for the welfare of farmers in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: A scheme for the welfare of farmers.

questions.question: What is the name of the health insurance scheme launched by the West Bengal government for its citizens?
options: Mukhyamantri Bradh Krishi Input Bima Yojana
questions.explanation: A health insurance scheme.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is a women empowerment program launched by the Government of West Bengal?
options: Mukhyamantri Bradh Krishi Input Bima Yojana
questions.explanation: A women empowerment program.

questions.question: What is the name of the door-to-door service launched by the West Bengal government for the delivery of government services?
options: Mukhyamantri Bradh Krishi Input Bima Yojana
questions.explanation: A door-to-door service.

questions.question: Which of the following is a crop insurance scheme launched by the Government of West Bengal for farmers?
options: Mukhyamantri Bradh Krishi Input Bima Yojana
questions.explanation: A crop insurance scheme.

questions.question: A shopkeeper in West Bengal sells an article at ₹ 800. If he made a profit of 25% on the cost price, what was the cost price of the article?
questions.explanation: Cost price is calculated by dividing selling price by 1 plus profit percentage.

questions.question: A person buys a share for ₹ 120 and sells it for ₹ 150. What is the percentage profit he made on the transaction?
questions.explanation: Profit percentage is calculated by finding the difference between selling and cost price, and then multiplying by 100.

questions.question: If a merchant in West Bengal incurs a loss of 12% on the selling of 1000 kg of rice, what amount of money will he get after selling the rice at the reduced price?
questions.explanation: Selling price is calculated by finding 100 minus loss percentage, then multiplying by the original selling price.

questions.question: A person buys a machine for 20% less than the marked price. If the marked price of the machine is ₹ 1200, what is the cost price of the machine?
questions.explanation: Cost price is calculated by finding 100 minus discount percentage, then multiplying by the marked price.

questions.question: A shopkeeper in West Bengal buys two articles for ₹ 500 each. If he sells the first article at a profit of 10% and the second article at a loss of 8%, what is his overall profit or loss?
questions.explanation: Profit or loss is calculated by finding the difference between the sum of the selling prices and the sum of the cost prices.

questions.question: A train travels from Sealdah to Howrah, a distance of 22 km. If the train's speed is 33 km/h, how much time will it take to cover this distance?
questions.explanation: Time = Distance / Speed.

questions.question: A car travels from Kolkata to Durgapur, a distance of 170 km in 5 hours. What is the average speed of the car?
questions.explanation: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time.

questions.question: A bus travels from Siliguri to Darjeeling, a distance of 75 km in 2.5 hours. What is the average speed of the bus?
questions.explanation: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time.

questions.question: A cyclist travels from Barasat to Barrackpore, a distance of 30 km in 2 hours. What is the average speed of the cyclist?
questions.explanation: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time.

questions.question: A taxi travels from Dumdum to Ballygunge, a distance of 18 km in 1.5 hours. What is the average speed of the taxi?
questions.explanation: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time.

questions.question: Moumita is the daughter of Sujata. Sujata is the wife of whom?
questions.explanation: Sujata is married to Sudipto.

questions.question: If I am the mother of Srijan, then Srijan's maternal grandmother is the sister of whom?
questions.explanation: Srijan's maternal grandmother is the sister of Arjun.

questions.question: Amit's mother is the sister of whom?
questions.explanation: Amit's mother is the sister of Nikhil.

questions.question: If I am the wife of Pritam, then I am the sister-in-law of whom?
questions.explanation: I am the sister-in-law of Koushik.

questions.question: Sayan is the son of whom?
questions.explanation: Sayan is the son of Pritam's brother.

questions.question: A person is standing in front of a statue of Swami Vivekananda. He turns 180 degrees and walks 20 meters, then turns left and walks 15 meters. He then turns right and walks 10 meters to a point. Which direction is he facing at the point?
questions.explanation: He turned 180 degrees in the beginning, so he is facing the opposite direction.

questions.question: A man is standing at the Howrah Bridge. He walks 150 meters towards the north, then turns 90 degrees to his right and walks 200 meters. He then turns 90 degrees to his right and walks 150 meters. Which direction is he facing at the end?
questions.explanation: After walking 200 meters, he turned right and walked 150 meters, which means he moved southwards.

questions.question: A boy is standing at the entrance of the Dakshineswar Kali Temple. He walks 50 meters straight, then turns 90 degrees to his left and walks 30 meters. He then turns 90 degrees to his right and walks 50 meters. Which direction is he facing at the end?
questions.explanation: After turning right and walking 50 meters, he moved eastwards.

questions.question: A man is standing at the Park Circus crossing. He walks 25 meters towards the east, then turns 180 degrees and walks 40 meters. He then turns 90 degrees to his right and walks 25 meters. Which direction is he facing at the end?
questions.explanation: After turning right, he moved northwards.

questions.question: A boy is standing at the Muktangan, Science city. He walks 75 meters towards the west, then turns 90 degrees to his left and walks 30 meters. He then turns 90 degrees to his right and walks 75 meters. Which direction is he facing at the end?
questions.explanation: After turning right and walking 75 meters, he moved eastwards.
